subject: Biology
title: The Great Wildebeest Migration
description: A quick, practical guide to The Great Wildebeest Migration.

hook.text: Every year, 1.5 million wildebeest cross the Serengeti—the largest land migration on Earth.

buildup.text: They follow the rains in a clockwise loop through Tanzania and Kenya, chasing fresh grass.

discovery.text: Crocodiles wait at river crossings. Lions patrol the plains. Thousands die. The herd moves on.

twist.text: The migration fertilizes the ecosystem—wildebeest droppings deposit nutrients across the savanna.

climax.text: If fences or climate change block the route, the entire ecosystem collapses.

punchline.text: 1.5 million animals chasing rain across a continent.

quiz.question: How many wildebeest migrate across the Serengeti each year?
